examples: 'stop_desc': 'POTOMAC YARD/VT METRO RAIL STATION SOUTH PAVILION ENTRANCE - EAST SIDE OF POTOMAC AVE & E.GLEBE RD INTERSECTION ' -> 'POTOMAC YARD/VT METRO RAIL STATION SOUTH PAVILION ENTRANCE - EAST SIDE OF POTOMAC AVE & E.GLEBE RD INTERSECTION'
'stop_desc': 'UNION STATION METRORAIL STATION NORTH ENTRANCE - 1ST ST NE & G ST NE ' -> 'UNION STATION METRORAIL STATION NORTH ENTRANCE - 1ST ST NE & G ST NE'
fixes.label: Trimmed surrounding whitespace

examples: ASHBURN METRORAIL STATION -> Ashburn Metrorail Station
LOUDOUN GATEWAY METRORAIL STATION -> Loudoun Gateway Metrorail Station
WASHINGTON DULLES INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT METRORAIL STATION -> Washington Dulles International Airport Metrorail Station
fixes.label: Recased shouting stop names

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 9955 of 9955 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 2026 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

findings.what: This feed describes station pathways, including step-free (elevator) routes.
findings.why: Riders can be routed through the station, and wheelchair users can see whether a step-free route exists.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (94% marked accessible, 6% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'number_out_of_range' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Number out of range (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'bidirectional_exit_gate' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Bidirectional exit gate (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already ended.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'same_name_and_description_for_stop' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Same name and description for stop (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Usually a re-export from your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Export and publish an updated GTFS feed that covers at least the next 30 days of service.
findings.what: This feed's service calendar runs out within the next 7 days.
findings.why: When the calendar ends, Google Maps and other trip planners drop your agency entirely. Riders see no service at all.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files leave out fields that GTFS asks for but does not require.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
findings.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
findings.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_window_outside_feed_period' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Service window outside feed period (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
findings.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
findings.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 13 kinds of issue across 8041 instances (5 error, 8028 warning, 8 informational).

findings.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
findings.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data ended 633 day(s) ago. Trip planners have likely already dropped this feed.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: This agency publishes realtime, but the feed needs an access key we don't have yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 2 validator errors to resolve. Service data expired 631 days ago. States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

fetch.user_agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Your feed's agency_id is 1; your National Transit Database ID is 30030. A feed that serves several agencies (a shared regional feed) can legitimately carry more than one agency_id, so a difference here is a heads-up, not an error.
ntd_id_alignment.fix: Optionally set the agency_id for your service to 30030 in agency.txt (and the matching agency_id in routes.txt) so the feed lines up with your NTD record. It is a convenience, not a required feed change: FTA also links agency_id to your NTD ID on your P-50 form.

pillars.detail: Service data expired 631 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

ntd_readiness.summary: Resolve this before you certify on the D-10. 2 validator errors to resolve. Service data expired 631 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 1 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (Green).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 418 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("VAN DORN ST METRORAIL STATION", "MORGAN BLVD METRORAIL STATION", "ADDISON RD METRORAIL STATION", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

top_fixes.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
top_fixes.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'number_out_of_range' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Number out of range (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'bidirectional_exit_gate' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Bidirectional exit gate (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.
